verses.latin_vulgate: Ne æmuleris viros malos,  nec desideres esse cum eis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּ֭קַנֵּא בְּאַנְשֵׁ֣י רָעָ֑ה וְאַל־תִּ֝תְאָ֗ו לִֽהְי֥וֹת אִתָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Seek not to be like evil men, neither desire to be with them:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä ole kiivas pahain ihmisten kanssa, ja älä himoitse olla heitä läsnä;
verses.king_james: Be not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia rapinas meditatur mens eorum,  et fraudes labia eorum loquuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־שֹׁ֭ד יֶהְגֶּ֣ה לִבָּ֑ם וְ֝עָמָ֗ל שִׂפְתֵיהֶ֥ם תְּדַבֵּֽרְנָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because their mind studieth robberies, and their lips speak deceits.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä heidän sydämensä pyytää vahingoittaa, ja heidän huulensa neuvovat pahuuteen.
verses.king_james: For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia ædificabitur domus,  et prudentia roborabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭חׇכְמָה יִבָּ֣נֶה בָּ֑יִת וּ֝בִתְבוּנָ֗ה יִתְכּוֹנָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: By wisdom the house shall be built, and by prudence it shall be strengthened.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisaudella raknnetaan huone, ja toimella se vahvistetaan.
verses.king_james: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established:

verses.latin_vulgate: In doctrina replebuntur cellaria,  universa substantia pretiosa et pulcherrima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּ֭בְדַעַת חֲדָרִ֣ים יִמָּלְא֑וּ כׇּל־ה֖וֹן יָקָ֣ר וְנָעִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: By instruction the storerooms shall be filled with all precious and most beautiful wealth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taidolla kammio täytetään kaikkinaisista kalliista ja jaloista tavaroista.
verses.king_james: And by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir sapiens fortis est,  et vir doctus robustus et validus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֶּבֶר־חָכָ֥ם בַּע֑וֹז וְאִֽישׁ־דַּ֝֗עַת מְאַמֶּץ־כֹּֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A wise man is strong: and a knowing man, stout and valiant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisas mies on väkevä, ja toimellinen mies on voimallinen väestä.
verses.king_james: A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia cum dispositione initur bellum,  et erit salus ubi multa consilia sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י בְ֭תַחְבֻּלוֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה־לְּךָ֣ מִלְחָמָ֑ה וּ֝תְשׁוּעָ֗ה בְּרֹ֣ב יוֹעֵֽץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because war is managed by due ordering: and there shall be safety where there are many counsels.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä neuvolla sodatkin pidetään: ja jossa monta neuvonantajaa on, siinä on voitto.
verses.king_james: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in multitude of counsellors there is safety.

verses.latin_vulgate: Excelsa stulto sapientia ;  in porta non aperiet os suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאמ֣וֹת לֶאֱוִ֣יל חׇכְמ֑וֹת בַּ֝שַּׁ֗עַר לֹ֣א יִפְתַּח־פִּֽיהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wisdom is too high for a fool; in the gate he shall not open his mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisaus on hullulle ylen korkia, ei hän tohdi avata suutansa portissa.
verses.king_james: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cogitat mala facere stultus vocabitur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְחַשֵּׁ֥ב לְהָרֵ֑עַ ל֝֗וֹ בַּעַל־מְזִמּ֥וֹת יִקְרָֽאוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that deviseth to do evils, shall be called a fool.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka ajattelee pahaa tehdäksensä, se kutsutaan pahain ajatusten päämieheksi.
verses.king_james: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person.

verses.latin_vulgate: cogitatio stulti peccatum est,  et abominatio hominum detractor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זִמַּ֣ת אִוֶּ֣לֶת חַטָּ֑את וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת לְאָדָ֣ם לֵֽץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The thought of a fool is sin: and the detractor is the abomination of men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tyhmäin ajatus on synti, ja pilkkaaja on kauhistus ihmisille.
verses.king_james: The thought of foolishness is sin: and the scorner is an abomination to men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si desperaveris lassus in die angustiæ,  imminuetur fortitudo tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭תְרַפִּיתָ בְּי֥וֹם צָרָ֗ה צַ֣ר כֹּחֶֽכָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: If thou lose hope, being weary in the day of distress, thy strength shall be diminished.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei se ole väkevä, joka ei tuskassa vahva ole.
verses.king_james: If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erue eos qui ducuntur ad mortem,  et qui trahuntur ad interitum, liberare ne cesses.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭צֵּל לְקֻחִ֣ים לַמָּ֑וֶת וּמָטִ֥ים לַ֝הֶ֗רֶג אִם־תַּחְשֽׂוֹךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Deliver them that are led to death: and those that are drawn to death, forbear not to deliver.
verses.finnish_biblia: Auta niitä, joita tappaa tahdotaan, ja älä vedä sinuas pois pois niiden tyköä, jotka kuoletetaan.
verses.king_james: If thou forbear to deliver them that are drawn unto death, and those that are ready to be slain;

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dixeris : Vires non suppetunt ;  qui inspector est cordis ipse intelligit :  et servatorem animæ tuæ nihil fallit,  reddetque homini juxta opera sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־תֹאמַ֗ר הֵן֮ לֹא־יָדַ֢עְנ֫וּ זֶ֥ה הֲֽלֹא־תֹ֘כֵ֤ן לִבּ֨וֹת ׀ הֽוּא־יָבִ֗ין וְנֹצֵ֣ר נַ֭פְשְׁךָ ה֣וּא יֵדָ֑ע וְהֵשִׁ֖יב לְאָדָ֣ם כְּפׇעֳלֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If thou say: I have not strength enough: he that seeth into the heart, he understandeth, and nothing deceiveth the keeper of thy soul, and he shall render to a man according to his works.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos sinä sanot: katso, emme ymmärrä sitä! luuletkos, ettei se, joka sydämet tutkii, sitä ymmärrä? ja se, joka sielustas ottaa vaarin, tunne sitä ja kosta ihmiselle tekonsa jälkeen?
verses.king_james: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?

verses.latin_vulgate: Comede, fili mi, mel, quia bonum est,  et favum dulcissimum gutturi tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱכׇל־בְּנִ֣י דְבַ֣שׁ כִּי־ט֑וֹב וְנֹ֥פֶת מָ֝ת֗וֹק עַל־חִכֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Eat honey, my son, because it is good, and the honeycomb most sweet to thy throat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Syö, poikani, hunajaa; sillä se on hyvä, ja mesileupä on makia suus laelle.
verses.king_james: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good; and the honeycomb, which is sweet to thy taste:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic et doctrina sapientiæ animæ tuæ :  quam cum inveneris, habebis in novissimis spem,  et spes tua non peribit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֤ן ׀ דְּעֶ֥ה חׇכְמָ֗ה לְנַ֫פְשֶׁ֥ךָ אִם־מָ֭צָאתָ וְיֵ֣שׁ אַחֲרִ֑ית וְ֝תִקְוָתְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תִכָּרֵֽת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: So also is the doctrine of wisdom to thy soul: which when thou hast found, thou shalt have hope in the end, and thy hope shall not perish.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on myös viisauden oppi sielulles: koskas sen löydät, niin sinun viimein käy hyvin, ja ei sinun toivos ole turha.
verses.king_james: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne insidieris, et quæras impietatem in domo justi,  neque vastes requiem ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֶּאֱרֹ֣ב רָ֭שָׁע לִנְוֵ֣ה צַדִּ֑יק אַֽל־תְּשַׁדֵּ֥ד רִבְצֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lie not in wait, nor seek after wickedness in the house of the just, nor spoil his rest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä vartioitse vanhurskaan huonetta, sinä jumalatoin, älä hukkaa hänen lepoansa.
verses.king_james: Lay not wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; spoil not his resting place:

verses.latin_vulgate: Septies enim cadet justus, et resurget :  impii autem corruent in malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י שֶׁ֨בַע ׀ יִפּ֣וֹל צַדִּ֣יק וָקָ֑ם וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים יִכָּשְׁל֥וּ בְרָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: For a just man shall fall seven times, and shall rise again: but the wicked shall fall down into evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä vanhurskas taitaa langeta seitsemän kertaa, ja nousee jälleen; mutta jumalattomat kaatuvat onnettomuuteen.
verses.king_james: For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ceciderit inimicus tuus ne gaudeas,  et in ruina ejus ne exsultet cor tuum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּנְפֹ֣ל (אויביך) [א֭וֹיִבְךָ] אַל־תִּשְׂמָ֑ח וּ֝בִכָּשְׁל֗וֹ אַל־יָגֵ֥ל לִבֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: When thy enemy shall fall, be not glad, and in his ruin let not thy heart rejoice:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä iloitse vihamiehes vahingosta, ja älköön sinun sydämes riemuitko hänen onnettomuudestansa,
verses.king_james: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne forte videat Dominus, et displiceat ei,  et auferat ab eo iram suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּן־יִרְאֶ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה וְרַ֣ע בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְהֵשִׁ֖יב מֵעָלָ֣יו אַפּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lest the Lord see, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ettei Herra näkisi sitä, ja se olis hänelle kelvotoin, ja hän kääntäis vihansa pois hänen päältänsä.
verses.king_james: Lest the Lord see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne contendas cum pessimis,  nec æmuleris impios :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתְחַ֥ר בַּמְּרֵעִ֑ים אַל־תְּ֝קַנֵּ֗א בָּֽרְשָׁעִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Contend not with the wicked, nor seek to be like the ungodly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä vihastu pahain päälle, älä myös ole kiivas jumalattomain tähden;
verses.king_james: Fret not thyself because of evil men, neither be thou envious at the wicked;

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam non habent futurorum spem mali,  et lucerna impiorum extinguetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י ׀ לֹא־תִהְיֶ֣ה אַחֲרִ֣ית לָרָ֑ע נֵ֖ר רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִדְעָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For evil men have no hope of things to come, and the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ei häijyllä ole mitään toivomista, ja jumalattomain kynttilä pitää sammuman.
verses.king_james: For there shall be no reward to the evil man; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Time Dominum, fili mi, et regem,  et cum detractoribus non commiscearis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְרָא־אֶת־יְהֹוָ֣ה בְּנִ֣י וָמֶ֑לֶךְ עִם־שׁ֝וֹנִ֗ים אַל־תִּתְעָרָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: My son, fear the Lord, and the king: and have nothing to do with detractors.
verses.finnish_biblia: Poikani, pelkää Herraa ja kuningasta, ja älä sekoita sinuas kapinan nostajain kanssa.
verses.king_james: My son, fear thou the Lord and the king: and meddle not with them that are given to change:

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam repente consurget perditio eorum,  et ruinam utriusque quis novit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־פִ֭תְאֹם יָק֣וּם אֵידָ֑ם וּפִ֥יד שְׁ֝נֵיהֶ֗ם מִ֣י יוֹדֵֽעַ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: For their destruction shall rise suddenly: and who knoweth the ruin of both?
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä heidän kadotuksensa nousee äkisti: ja kuka tietää, koska kummankin onnetomuus tulee?
verses.king_james: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc quoque sapientibus.   Cognoscere personam in judicio non est bonum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־אֵ֥לֶּה לַחֲכָמִ֑ים הַֽכֵּר־פָּנִ֖ים בְּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט בַּל־טֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: These things also to the wise: It is not good to have respect to persons in judgment.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nämä ovat myös viisasten: ei ole hyvä katsoa muotoa tuomiossa.
verses.king_james: These things also belong to the wise. It is not good to have respect of persons in judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dicunt impio : Justus es : maledicent eis populi,  et detestabuntur eos tribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹ֤מֵ֨ר ׀ לְרָשָׁע֮ צַדִּ֢יק אָ֥֫תָּה יִקְּבֻ֥הוּ עַמִּ֑ים יִזְעָמ֥וּהוּ לְאֻמִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that say to the wicked man: Thou art just: shall be cursed by the people, and the tribes shall abhor them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka jumalattomalle sanoo: sinä olet hurskas! häntä kiroovat ihmiset, ja kansa vihaa häntä.
verses.king_james: He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui arguunt eum laudabuntur,  et super ipsos veniet benedictio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַמּוֹכִיחִ֥ים יִנְעָ֑ם וַ֝עֲלֵיהֶ֗ם תָּב֥וֹא בִרְכַּת־טֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that rebuke him shall be praised: and a blessing shall come upon them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jotka rankaisevat, he ovat otolliset, ja runsas siunaus tulee heidän päällensä.
verses.king_james: But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Labia deosculabitur  qui recta verba respondet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂפָתַ֥יִם יִשָּׁ֑ק מֵ֝שִׁ֗יב דְּבָרִ֥ים נְכֹחִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: He shall kiss the lips, who answereth right words.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toimellinen vastaus on niinkuin suloinen suun antamus.
verses.king_james: Every man shall kiss his lips that giveth a right answer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præpara foris opus tuum,  et diligenter exerce agrum tuum,  ut postea ædifices domum tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָ֘כֵ֤ן בַּח֨וּץ ׀ מְלַאכְתֶּ֗ךָ וְעַתְּדָ֣הּ בַּשָּׂדֶ֣ה לָ֑ךְ אַ֝חַ֗ר וּבָנִ֥יתָ בֵיתֶֽךָ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Prepare thy work without, and diligently till thy ground: that afterward thou mayst build thy house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toimita työs ulkona, ja valmista peltos, ja rakenna sitte huonees.
verses.king_james: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne sis testis frustra contra proximum tuum,  nec lactes quemquam labiis tuis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּהִ֣י עֵד־חִנָּ֣ם בְּרֵעֶ֑ךָ וַ֝הֲפִתִּ֗יתָ בִּשְׂפָתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Be not witness without cause against thy neighbour: and deceive not any man with thy lips.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä todista lähimmäistäs vastaan ilman syytä, ja älä petä suullas.
verses.king_james: Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and deceive not with thy lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dicas : Quomodo fecit mihi, sic faciam ei ;  reddam unicuique secundum opus suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֹּאמַ֗ר כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר עָֽשָׂה־לִ֭י כֵּ֤ן אֶֽעֱשֶׂה־לּ֑וֹ אָשִׁ֖יב לָאִ֣ישׁ כְּפׇעֳלֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Say not: I will do to him as he hath done to me: I will render to every one according to his work.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä sano: niinkuin hän teki minulle, niin minä teen hänelle: minä kostan jokaiselle hänen tekonsa jälkeen.
verses.king_james: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me: I will render to the man according to his work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per agrum hominis pigri transivi,  et per vineam viri stulti :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־שְׂדֵ֣ה אִישׁ־עָצֵ֣ל עָבַ֑רְתִּי וְעַל־כֶּ֝֗רֶם אָדָ֥ם חֲסַר־לֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: I passed by the field of the slothful man, and by the vineyard of the foolish man:
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä kävin laiskan pellon ohitse, ja tyhmän viinamäen sivuitse,
verses.king_james: I went by the field of the slothful, and by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;

verses.latin_vulgate: et ecce totum repleverant urticæ,  et operuerant superficiem ejus spinæ,  et maceria lapidum destructa erat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֨ה עָ֘לָ֤ה כֻלּ֨וֹ ׀ קִמְּשֹׂנִ֗ים כׇּסּ֣וּ פָנָ֣יו חֲרֻלִּ֑ים וְגֶ֖דֶר אֲבָנָ֣יו נֶהֱרָֽסָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold it was all filled with nettles, and thorns had covered the face thereof, and the stone wall was broken down.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, siinä olivat paljaat ohdakkeet kasvaneet, ja se oli nukulaista täynnä, ja aidat olivat kaatuneet.
verses.king_james: And, lo, it was all grown over with thorns, and nettles had covered the face thereof, and the stone wall thereof was broken down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum vidissem, posui in corde meo,  et exemplo didici disciplinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶחֱזֶ֣ה אָ֭נֹכִי אָשִׁ֣ית לִבִּ֑י רָ֝אִ֗יתִי לָקַ֥חְתִּי מוּסָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which when I had seen, I laid it up in my heart, and by the example I received instruction.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin minä sen bäin, panin minä sen sydämeeni, katselin ja opin siitä.
verses.king_james: Then I saw, and considered it well: I looked upon it, and received instruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Parum, inquam, dormies, modicum dormitabis ;  pauxillum manus conseres ut quiescas :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְעַ֣ט שֵׁ֭נוֹת מְעַ֣ט תְּנוּמ֑וֹת מְעַ֓ט ׀ חִבֻּ֖ק יָדַ֣יִם לִשְׁכָּֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou wilt sleep a little, said I, thou wilt slumber a little, thou wilt fold thy hands a little to rest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä tahdot vielä vähä maata ja unelias olla, ja enempi käsiäs yhteen panna lepäämään;
verses.king_james: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

verses.latin_vulgate: et veniet tibi quasi cursor egestas,  et mendicitas quasi vir armatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָֽא־מִתְהַלֵּ֥ךְ רֵישֶׁ֑ךָ וּ֝מַחְסֹרֶ֗יךָ כְּאִ֣ישׁ מָגֵֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And poverty shall come to thee as a runner, and beggary as an armed man.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta köyhyytes pitää sinulle tuleman niinkuin matkamies, ja vaivaisuus niinkuin varustettu mies.
verses.king_james: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth; and thy want as an armed man.
